title: Per-cycle audit must fail a cycle that adds an exported symbol with no naming test / 0% coverage — repo-wide apicover per-package hard-fail is the delayed detector, and it turned main RED after cycle-960 shipped 3 orphaned internal/core exports
why: ROOT-CAUSE UPDATE 2026-07-21 (2nd CI red from this reproduction): the shipped test MUTATES THE LIVE REPO TREE (creates internal/apicoverreprofixture998 in-tree, runs `make -C go apicover` which regenerates coverage.txt mid-CI-run, then deletes the package) — poisoning the CI coverage profile with a nonexistent package and killing `go tool cover -func` on BOTH platforms ('fork/exec: invalid argument'). Now FULLY DISABLED via top-of-test t.Skip (commit pending). THE FIX CYCLE MUST: (a) rebuild the reproduction against a throwaway TEMP COPY of the module, never the live tree; (b) point composedGateTargets[apicover] at an enforcing recipe; (c) flip the assertion to t.Fatalf as the regression pin. 4TH INSTANCE + RED-TEST-SHIPPED 2026-07-21: cycle-998 landed the TDD reproduction (cmd_composition_apicover_gap_test.go + internal/apicoverreprofixture998) WITHOUT the fix, holding main RED; operator converted the failure branch to a loud t.Skipf tripwire at the boundary — THE FIX MUST flip it back to t.Fatalf as its regression pin. The reproduction also nails the mechanism: composedGateTargets binds gate 'apicover' to the Phase-0 WARNING-ONLY Makefile target (never -enforce), so the fleet-rebase carry-forward fast path reships without the enforcing check CI runs. Cycle-960 (504bc913) added carryforward_filter.go + prune_superseded_orphans.go exporting CarryforwardCandidateLandable, PruneSupersededOrphans, and the OrphanVerdict type — with ZERO tests naming them. The cycle SHIPPED: the per-cycle audit's CI-parity apicover check runs CHANGED-SCOPE (only symbols in the diff, plus a new-package graduation grace) and green-lit it. But the repo-wide `go` workflow enforces apicover PER-PACKAGE HARD-FAIL (every exported symbol in the package), so on push main's `go` workflow went RED ('UNCOVERED (no test names it): 3'). This is the recurring warnship_apicover_ci_gap class (#288/#313, ADR-0069): the per-cycle gate and the repo-wide gate disagree on scope, so a cycle can ship green and STILL red-line main — which then blocks EVERY subsequent PR (universal-fallback #342 and the scout-goalhash fix both inherited the red) and stalls the operator's 4-ship release gate. Delayed detection = the whole point of the parity gate (ADR-0069) is defeated for apicover specifically.
how_to_apply: Close the scope gap in the per-cycle audit's apicover check (internal/phases/audit/ciparity.go apicoverEnforceChanged* path): when a cycle's diff ADDS an exported symbol to a package that is already on the .apicover-enforce list, run the SAME per-package hard-fail apicover the repo-wide `go` workflow runs on that touched package (named-by-test AST AND >0% executed coverage, -tags integration), not just changed-scope. A newly-exported-but-unnamed/uncovered symbol must FAIL the cycle audit at build time — before ship — so the author adds the naming test in-cycle. Reuse bin/apicover -enforce; the touched-package list already exists in the audit. Keep the new-package graduation grace for brand-new packages not yet on the enforce list, but a symbol added to an ALREADY-enforced package gets no grace.
notes: [MERGE 2026-07-23] Absorbs percycle-apicover-gate-missed-new-exports-fleet-rebase-path; carried AC: after fleet-rebase, re-derive the changed-set from the REBASED worktree before the apicover audit — per-package hard-fail cannot fire on an empty changed-set.
